mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in FEDFUNDS over the trailing three months signals an active monetary tightening cycle that raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX 20-day forward returns during rising-FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points over a single month, this discrete deterioration in labor market slack signals weakening consumer income and corporate earnings prospects, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In more than 55% of historical triggers, SPX 20-day forward returns are positive, or the signal's rank IC is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) jointly indicate a broad-based contraction in consumer and housing demand that is not captured by either series alone, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: The joint-decline signal fails to outperform either single-series signal in out-of-sample rank IC, or SPX 60-day forward returns following joint declines are not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion where GDPC1 growth remains positive, acceleration in INDPRO (industrial production) month-over-month growth signals continued supply-side capacity utilization that supports corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This relationship is expected to weaken or reverse outside late-cycle regimes.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth accelerates for two consecutive months
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with positive GDPC1 growth
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO acceleration flips sign or falls below 0.03 during late-cycle windows, or positive returns are not observed in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects continued labor market expansion, which investors interpret as a near-term signal of sustained consumer spending power, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets react to the employment print.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS prints are not significantly higher than following negative prints (t-test p > 0.05), or rank IC falls below 0.02 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls below its trailing 12-month low, this threshold breach signals a structural downturn in housing demand that historically precedes broader economic slowdowns, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its trailing 12-month minimum
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns after HOUST breaches its 12-month low are not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or fewer than 50% of historical breaches are followed by negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: The net equity market effect of rising INDPRO depends on whether FEDFUNDS is also rising: when industrial production growth outpaces the rate of monetary tightening (INDPRO momentum exceeds FEDFUNDS change in standardized terms), the net effect is positive for S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days; when tightening dominates, the effect reverses to negative. This is a genuine interaction not decomposable into either series alone.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month annualized growth exceeds FEDFUNDS 3-month change in standardized units
failure_condition: The interaction term's marginal contribution to rank IC is not statistically distinguishable from the sum of single-series effects, or the sign of the relationship is unstable across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening regime, an acceleration in CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation increases market expectations of further Fed rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets reprice near-term policy risk. In a stable or easing regime, the same inflation acceleration has no reliable directional effect.
failure_condition: During tightening regimes, SPX 5-day forward returns show no significant negative relationship to CPIAUCSL YoY acceleration (p > 0.05), or the effect direction is not distinguishable from the non-tightening regime.
